operation: First, a 2D sketch is drawn on the XZ workplane: start at coordinate (0, 5), draw a vertical line down to (0, 0), create a three-point arc passing through the midpoint (3, -2) that ends at (6, 0), draw a vertical line up to (6, 5), close the sketch to form a closed profile, then extrude this 2D profile 4 units along the Y-axis to generate the final 3D solid.
geometry: This is a uniform-thickness prismatic solid, with a shield-shaped cross-section that has a straight horizontal top edge, two parallel equal-length vertical side edges, and a downward convex curved bottom edge connecting the bottom ends of the two vertical sides. All cross-sections along the extrusion direction are identical, with flat rectangular side faces corresponding to the straight edges of the profile and a single curved side face corresponding to the bottom arc of the profile.
function: This shield-shaped prismatic component is intended for use as a small protective guard, decorative emblem base, or lightweight mounting bracket, featuring a smooth curved lower profile suitable for impact absorption or aesthetic styling and uniform construction for consistent low-load structural performance.
